changelog:
Counter visibility — verify credits/tokens flow end-to-end.

User reported the dashboard's Credits / Credits Total / Tokens cards were not moving. To diagnose without guessing, this release adds one INFO-level log line per successful request that shows the entire counter chain:

  [Stats] model=claude-sonnet-4.5 key=<id> in=234 out=512 credits=1.5000 total_req=42 total_tok=15822 total_cred=63.20

If you make a /v1/messages, /v1/chat/completions, or /v1/responses request and that line does NOT appear in the container logs, then recordSuccess is not being reached — check upstream Kiro accepted the request (no 401/403/402/429), filters are not stripping the prompt, and the model is in the account's whitelist. If the line DOES appear but the dashboard cards still don't move, hard-refresh the browser (the 10-second poll might be stalled) and check /admin/api/status directly:

  curl http://localhost:8989/admin/api/status -H "X-Admin-Password: ..."

The response should include totalRequests, totalTokens, totalCredits, quotaTotal, quotaUsed; the dashboard's Credits card prefers quotaUsed (account-true, sum of usageCurrent across accounts) and falls back to totalCredits when no account quota is loaded yet.

If totalTokens moves but totalCredits stays 0, your Kiro accounts are not emitting meteringEvent for the model you're using. Older or free-tier accounts may skip the metering event; the proxy can only forward what Kiro reports.

Set LOG_LEVEL=warn in production once you've verified the chain.

A18: production-readiness — panic recovery, CI tests, spec compat fixes.
A17: review pass — security hardening + dead-code purge + UX bugs.
A16: real per-key limit enforcement + /v1/responses tracking.
A15: legacy fast-path tracking gap + datetime-local roundtrip.
A14: persistent SQLite statistics.
A13: highly customizable api keys.
A12: unify api keys.
A11: WebSocket transport.
A10: dashboard API Keys + Analytics tabs.
A9: entrypoint chown + su-exec hotfix.
A8: dashboard credits use account-true quotaUsed.
A7: backend multi API keys.
A6: security + reliability hardening.
